star_1.text: The login process to this app is ridiculous. Even though I set it up with a password and an email it keeps on emailing me a code that I have to enter in. Every single time. And, It's impossible to figure out how to track future flights. If it's not happening at the moment It can't be found. Also, when I am trying to look up a flight it thinks I'm logging in and so it retrieves my password and puts it in for the flight number. This app is useless.

It seems to be a great app at first, paid to remove ads. Unfortunately when i was trying to register as new user. I kept getting the same generic error message, saying 'the username name is unavailable'. I have tried multiple options, and still getting the same result. At least tell me what went wrong in error message, so that I can fix it. Very frustrating for app I just paid for. And without login to the app, I can't even use 'flight alert'. Think twice if you're getting this app.

Like so many apps now, wants to bombard you with notifications. however, unlike other apps, it's impossible to use unless you agree to receive notifications. The programmers have set it so there's no way to get past the "want notifications" pop-up without agreeing to notifications.

Trying app for first time and trying to set notifications for a flight scheduled later today (29th). After I set the notifications the flight that gets saved is for the previous day and has already arrived. It will not set the notification for the correct date.

Using Flightaware for ages on a PC and it's the app of my choice - on a computer... In comparison, the app is a disaster. First, logging in wasn't possible. I was told to check my email for a code but nothing ever arrived. (A procedure by the way, that works fine on a PC.) So I started without being logged in, searched for a specific flight (that is currently on my PC screen), but the map in the app was completely blank. Or I better say, white. And finally I found the delete button for this app.
the aircraft usually never load. the maps just stays empty

I'm trying to track a flight for tomorrow, there is no way to change a date, not even a area to enter a date at all, horrible!! Trying it again, now it shows the flights for the next day, but now there is no way to delete the flights. This is a very simple thing that desperately needed, what's wrong with developers lmao, do they have the heads completely up there a.. ??

Great until it wasn't. Paid for no ads and was working fine on my Android, then it just quit working one day. No a/c displayed and I live in a very active flight area. Then couldn't even log on. Started getting communication errors. Forced stop & restarted. Nothing. Cleared app cache. Nothing. Uninstalled and reinstalled app. Still nothing. On cusp of just giving up and uninstalling permanently. Did I mention support is non-existant?

Used to be user friendly and now....NOT If you put in a flight # it gives you yours and EVERY flight with that number. You can't track the progress.. Either it looks like the plane is still at the departure or it's already arrived when it hasn't. It used to be a good app now I have to find another

Worth App ever. Every time I track a flight it arrives late or early, it never gives an accurate arriving time. I understand that ETA is not accurate but with this App the real arriving time is also not accurate. You have to wait sometimes one hour for the aircraft to land and sometimes the passenger has to wait for you to arrive because you trused this piece of....... that displayed that the aircraft needs also 50 minutes to land.

Recently included an email MFA, which is fine. However, every time I receive the code and enter it, I get "Error: failed to fetch provider options." This is a great app, based on previous use, but I cannot even get into it anymore. Hoping to get technical assistance with this review. Will update if/when this is resolved.

This app no longer works. I've tried reinstalling, but it just doesn't work AT ALL. I live in the flight path to Sea-Tac airport and have a small airport near me with planes flying overhead about 500 feet about my house. It was fun to see what airline or what type of plane it is, but now I'm uninstalling because the app is useless.

App had been on phone for years and worked fine. Now when I open it nothing works. The aircraft I track is there but thats it. I have uninstalled the app shut the phone off back on reinstalled app and it's as if I never uninstalled the app the aircraft I track is already there. The Logout tab does nothing. If I go to my laptop all is good.

Worthless. You can't make heads or tails on how the information is reported. When you do find your flight you are looking for and click on the the status of incoming flight, nothing happens. Do yourself a favor and use the carrier app as it has more accurate information and is much easier to understand. Uninstalled.

Gettig worse. this app used to be great but is now consistently buggy and displays incorrect data. notifications dont work, ever, and never have. Support dont seem interested in fixing bugs and j have reported multiple. Tigerair Australia flights more iften than not show Qantas data and flight information and the support teams tried to say it was a codeshare agreement 😅 i wouldn't recommend this for a download until issues are fixed.

Used this all the time when traveling until the pandemic hit. I'm trying to use it again now, and it is buggy and not as intuitive. Can't search for flights based on date, and it doesn't save the correct flights. I'm going to remove this and use one of the competitor's apps.

What a piece of $#it. Can't find any info about meeting a flight ARRIVING at the selected airport, you have to go to the source city and find the flight using a departure time you don't have. And, by the way... PAY! PAY! PAY! PAY! No, I'm not going to pay for this garbage app. Not going to happen. DELETE!

Terrible. Since smart phones came on the market, this is probably the first negative review that I have ever given. If you ever need password recovery for this app, you can forget it. The email will never come, and support is dismissive and totally useless. Better to choose another flight tracker app.

App stopped working about a month ago. Doesn't show airports, flights, routes... I have uninstalled and reinstalled it several times to no avail. Any word on what is happening? The issue doesn't get a mention on their website, or in google searches

Picking up customers in a few hours and trying to plan my journey. Used this app; but a waste of time as the flight should leave in minutes and the app shows the flight as landed months ago. If you can't update in time there is no point for the app.

star_2.text: Barely has information I need - not all information I'd like. Tends to freeze during flight tracking. Rarely do I prefer the desktop version but in this case I do. ----------------- OK, 2 years later ... web version of FlightAware is still a better choice. Quicker, more informative ... and doesn't use system resources when not in use. Not sorry, still barely 2 stars.

Was once a great app. Now super difficult to use. 1) There's a blocked plane I can't remove from my aircraft. No matter how many times I delete it, the aircraft returns. 2) The app never stays logged in. I must log in at each use of it, and the new log in process is a painful three step process, all for protecting unimportant information such as the list of planes I track.

When it decides to work, it's great. The "verifying your a human" regularly hangs. App recognises my email address but I never receive the authentication code. And yeah, I check the spam folder -- it just never arrives. People need to stop tinkering with their apps, it usually doesn't seem to work out well.

It never shows when the flight is delayed. Always mention on time, scheduled etc. Even a Google search can give you better information. However I like the way it shows the number of hours elapsed etc. detail and it usually shows the landing time correctly.

Used to be fine,but now it's not letting me choose the flight I want to track - I select a flight for tomorrow and it gives me one that's arrived today. Why would I want to track a flight that's already landed - I can get that from the airport. Hope it's just a year end glitch.

Why does your app keep signing me out every day? Why can't we stay signed in? And why do you make it SOOO hard to sign in (login, then emailed code, THEN authenticator code) It's a flight tracker, not a crypto wallet... Come on. What are you protecting? Why don't you implement biometrics like my bank app, do I can login without needing to go to 2 other apps, just to login to add an alert for a flight?

This app has become inconvenient to use due to the increased security features. I have no confidential information on the app. Why does it have THREE step verification? It's easier to log into my bank account at this point. If I only had to log in once, I would not have bothered complaining about it, but I have to go through THREE step verification nearly every time I use it.
works much differently on an android vs an iPhone, the app should have the same features for both

Whenever I heard or saw a plane going overhead, I'd check the app to see where it's going to or coming from and see what the weather is like for any given destination. It appears the weather conditions aren't there anymore....Too many planes show up that aren't really there and don't even fly here, Africa One, Chinese flying dragon airlines, after a Google search those are local airlines in China and Africa. Tapping on them obviously comes up "retrieval failed"

Another great app for people who love logging in to apps. You can't log in without being emailed a code, which takes ages to appear, and trying to enable MFA results in an error. Not a great user experience. I'd comment on the functionality of the app, but I've not been able to login yet.

Hmmm. Looking for FlightRadar alternative. Lacks quite a few important bits. Seem to have to login relentlessly when I haven't logged out. Ridiculous. Losing another star for its inability to stay logged in AND requiring email code, copy & paste. Far too cumbersome. Paid my "remove ad" option but nothing to tell you the huge fee jump to save more items. No option for "Map screen first" like FR. Very annoying. Arrivals/departures are good but cluttered in comparison to FR. Had higher hopes.

Can sometimes show more aircraft than the Flightradar24 app. But this app is a bit slower to load, and doesn't seem to have aircraft info tags, so you have to click on each aircraft to see what it is, which becomes tedious. If you could have aircraft type tags showing all the time would be a reason to use this over Flightradar24.

App would be great if it worked completely. Tracking my son's flight shows location but none of the "More Details" information. Same app, same flight on wife's iOS works fine, shows all details. So, one of those cases where one platform is favored over another.

This used to be the best app, but recently you can no longer search by flight number. You enter the flight number and even the airline and hit search and nothing happens. You can search by route but that's annoying because all airlines come up. What happened???

Giving it 2-stars for the extremely poor flight tracking. More often than not the app loses track of the flight and shows "estimated" for almost the entire journey. This is despite other competing apps clearly showing the plane is still trackable. This causes unnecessary anxiety as we as users would fear the worst for our loved ones. Definitely not recommended.

I used to use this app all the time to track family's planes coming in. I just used it in March with no problem. Now today I tried to use it to track my daughter's flight and it no longer allows me to do in app push notifications and when I try to sign up for email alerts it says that I'm not registered. This was the best part of the app.

When the app works I love it. Paid for the key to have more features. BUT...lately it just loses everything, forgets searches, freezes half way throught a flight (my husband's a pilot so I follow his flights). The only way to get it going again is to uninstall the app and reinstall it.

Getting less useful with each update. Just like with several other apps that I use and were originally very good, the developer with each update screws up the app. For example, I used to use this app to see helicopter traffic in my area now that is non-existent. And as others have attested to, the notifications dont always work. I have begun to hate updates anymore, they may fix some bugs, but they always introduce more and usually bigger ones. It's so stupid. I will probably not use it anymor

The app is great, BUT somehow it forces me to log in every time. I followed my wife's flight to pick her up at the airport and every time I opened the app it wanted me to sign in to view "my flights". SUPER annoying since it's multifactor via email AND authenticator. Thus reducing from t to only 2 stars!

Usually when you download an app, you expect more functionality than the website. The opposite is true here. For example you cannot see a planes registration in the app but you can on the website. Full of annoying ads. Removing and going back to the web version.

star_3.text: Being a chauffeur, this app has been really helpful! 😁 ...but sometime within the last month I can no longer see the recent searches unless I'm connected to Wi-Fi, which makes it harder to keep up to date shuttling pilots & crew. 🙁 Plus, apparently a few pilots have told me they're recently having problems, but I can't remember what those are (I just remember they're different issues than mine).

App is generally functional; however, when viewing any given aircraft on the map, the position does not continuously update as it does in a web browser. In order to get an updated position, you have to refresh the page, usually multiple times. Also, whenever you select a flight, the map starts zoomed way out so you can see North and South America, instead of showing a more localized view of the aircraft and route.
good app for tracking your flight and its very easy

The app shows a bunch of info, including flights on the map. But the main reason why I payed for the app was to get notifications when a flight arrives or gets delayed. And these notifications NEVER work. Never. All permissions are granted, alerts for a particular flight configured, and yet it's quiet. No notifications never. Bummer.

Good app. Simple and easy to use. Information accurate for most of the part. Got in on my personal and work phones. But recently the connection between the app and then server ceased to work. Reinstalled couple of times, same issue always. Finally i contacted support who told me the app will not work anymore on androids below 9.

Not very accurate with arrival times so don't depend on this if you're picking someone up at the airport. But what's really annoying is that the alerts don't get sorted by date on the app but by the order in which they are added. Surely this is a simple enough fix? It'll get some semblance of agreement with website functionality if you can fix this.

Liking most functions of this app, just three sticky points stopping me purchasing the ad-free key: 1. When a flight is delayed should also show the original scheduled time for reference. 2. Should also show shared short codes in the flight details & departure/arrival boards as your flight may not be with the main carrier. 3. Should be able to bookmark a future flight more than 24hrs in advance. All are done in other flight trackers so it is possible...

I use this app at work. I paid for the yearly subscription several months ago. Now ads are showing up at the bottom of the screen. I thought that the yearly subscription would remove all ads. I would have given it 4 stars had that not happened.

First thing I noticed as a new user is that the information at the top of the result screens and the settings page is hidden by the header bar, making it difficult to view some results, and leaving some settings inaccessible. This is on a Pixel 7 Pro.

it used to be a great app, now it hardly ever works. search apps working. try logging out, then go to your phone settings and clear all data from app. then log back in. don't understand why the data gets corrupted so easily.

I find that Flightaware can be useful at times but sometimes the app displays wring information. For example I was tracking Malaysia Airlines flight 2 from KL to London which displays the service is flown by an A388 in fact when I move on to flightradar24 it displays the the service is flown by an A350, can you guys fix this problem?
Seems like a decent option for tracking flights in the next day but there is a limit of only tracking a few flights at a time. Not a good free option.
It's provided a great service over the years, but lately the app has just not worked well. I have to log in every time I open the app, which is also causing my alerts not to come through.

Does not offer the same functionality as the website. Speed and altitude are not displayed on the map next to the miniature airplane in the app. So I now went back to using the website. The app needs improvement.

Ok app but why the feature differential between Android and IOS? Could really use the sort and filter options on Android. And why are the arrival/departure times vastly different between the two OS's? Isn't the information being pulled from the same source?

After searching and installing half a dozen other flight tracking apps, this is the first one I found that doesn't force me to rotate my tablet to Portrait. Will upgrade the rating if warranted after using today. Thank You!
It tracks planes pretty good but the constant being signed out of the app and having to re-log in while I'm driving or in a hurry and in need of immediate flight tracking is to much.

The latest update took away the manual refresh, thus making it very hard to update if try to update the same area. Also, I have noticed some aircraft not showing that were showing previously. Hope this can be fixed with the next update.

Great app...on the web and iOS. Android app is getting there. App will take a lot of system resources and crash if you use the Track Inbound Flight option to go back in time more than just a few previous flights.

I love and use the app daily for work and I show in my widgets that it has A WIDGET, but when I go to use it, "application not installed on phone" displays. Is this a paid service or an issue?? Provide me that, and it's four stars!

star_4.text: Good for free, some bugs. Two main ones I experienced are 1. In the Android app, if you change an alert to be for only one day, the summary still shows that it is alerting you indefinitely. 2. In the website, you can say how much of a heads-up you need on a flight. The default is 45 minutes, and you can change it, but if you "edit" again in order to see if your change stuck, it still shows 45 minutes.

Good app! Always room for improvement. Very easy to use. Would like to be able to connect device to monitor or TV to see flight tracking info. Currently only see startup script. Would also like feature that allows you to hear flight chatter. (I currently use a separate radio for that). Lastly, would like a feature that allows a camera installation to capture pics of flights as they pass overhead. I live very near an active runway and military base.

Works for the most part but the user interface could use a lot of work. Won't let me remove an item from "My Aircraft," a flight I'm tracking won't refresh, and just a clunky user experience overall. 2024/01/24 Updating to 4 stars. My review is probably a bit harsh. I guess I've become used to the interface and have come to rely on this app whenever we travel.

For free, its a Nice app to check Gates, Terminals, Departing time change, of your commercial flights. However there is some small interface issues, not to friendly. Like if you tracking by registration number, it will track ok, then I tried to see details of the aircraft, like pictures, but can find details. Since I have an iphone too, i can tell, The Android version is better and freedom than the iphone.

Super powerful if you want to notified, by push notification, email to yourself, or email to someone else, about any event of a specified flight. It's also the only app I could find that does this without having to get a paid subscription. Only feedback is that the default ("classic") map type in the "Nearby me" tab is just really low resolution, which lead me to change to the "street" map type, which is essentially Google Maps, but it isn't designed or optimized to have a flights overlay.

I use it when my dear ones fly, it gives me a good feeling to know where they are, how long before arriving and most of all relief when I see " landed". I remember when I used it for the first time for my son's long flight and the green line stopped in the middle of the ocean for a couple of hours...😀...I was terrified...until it started again. At that moment I noticed the small letters note that said the radar canno.7t track the flights in some areas. Thank you for "flightaware"

Best tracker I have found in the app store. There is a website, but I prefer the app. Intuitive and works. Don't travel enough to get a subscription but would pay a one time purchase that is reasonable. Please consider. Otherwise, I'll just deal with ads, which aren't too bad.

Have had this app for years. Really love it for the most part. Simple to use for a (normally) frequent flyer. Only two issues noted. 1.) The map display has become pretty cluttered showing all the private planes in the air. I'd like to see an option to be able to see just commercial/ cargo flights. 2.) Boeing jets don't always show correctly. Show up as "BBJ-3" alot of times instead of the actual plane type. Otherwise love the app!!

I use this app probably about 4 to 6 times a day due to my job. I think that this is the best one out there. The reason why I do not rate this 5 stars is the "alert's" feature. If it's short notice and I tried to use the alert, it wants to set it for the next day and not the current one. Hopefully someday we'll get fixed. If they were to fix that I would make it five stars

Nice app easy to use and find flights. One major bug.. if i want to track a future flight, even later same day, usually the second leg of a multi.... I can find the flight fine, but the list of flights that shows up later shows the same # flight from yesterday instead. They should fix that.

Once again, Ads have come back. I paid for the ad removal back when it was one time fee, and have not seen any email or communication about it. Edit: this seems to be a bug with Google play and flight aware. Uninstall and reinstall fixed it, again. Tired of being required to do this once a year

Best free version of a flight tracker + most up-to-date data. A few months ago, I knew my flight had aborted its landing & diverted to a nearby airport during a severe thunderstorm...30 MIN BEFORE United even notified the flight was delayed. A must have app for frequent OR occasional travelers.

Pretty good for the most part. I use it for work and today for example, it says that the flight is simultaneously headed to BDL and ROC in the flight schedule. Also, using the radar map from My Airports shows the plan on the correct path but using the map on My Aircraft shows it hundreds of miles off course. Another bug is when you refresh the flight list after selecting an airport and flights no longer appear. Sorting could be better with arrival and departure times not always chronological.

Cool app to see what's flying over your head. It's a little hard to use the way we use it... we like to drop research into the tail numbers and see a lot more features than this app provides, but it's a great little starting point for aviation heads.

This is a really good app for tracking flights, especially if your on the end of a flight to pick some one up at the airport. Two critics: needs to have the ability to use a 24 hour clock and translate time zones. I'm in EDT and trying to figure out the time conversions isn't that easy. IE: Dallas is in CDT, so what is the equal I'm my time zone?

Its pretty good for a free app. I just wish it was a little easier to find specific flights. Sometimes its real hard or nonexistent to find flights that I know that people are on. If they could fix that it'd be 5 stars.

Its a good app, love aviation, and live close to airport. Like to track them leaving and coming in. But sometimes I see planes leave or going over my house, and their not on the map. Is it because of they're a private flight, or is there transponder not on?
Very good if you're a flight sim enthusiast and want to copy real world flight plans. (providing the flight plan has been filed on the app) this isn't always the case.

Great fun to use. I never have the Login issue that others have commented on. I never logged-in in the first place... it just shows me the flight traffic of the local or chosen airport. (Maybe I need to login to track a particular flight?)
Been using for years. After latest update, app freezes, or loads very slowly. Also, the ads now take up half the screen, & use tons of data. Please fix.

star_5.text: The very best of the free flight trackers. The flight search is intuitive and useful. You don't have to know arcane airline codes or airport codes. You can just use the airline name and flight number, and it prompts you with a list to pick from. You can mark multiple flights as favorites so you can track them all from one page. I've tried a couple of other flight trackers with better ratings or more downloads. This one is better.

Tried others that did not work but had 4.5 stars and higher. This one was very quick and very easy. Put in airline and flight number pulled up everything I needed and didn't ask for a rating or membership when I opened it either.
Easy to use & tracking. Excellent 🤍
very good app, I will definitely let my friends know that this can help you when you travel, anywhere.

i love this app. not only do i use it to track flights if lived ones, but i use it to just watch flights sometimes. and see what their air speed is and altitude. if you zoom all the way out and look at all the flights crossing the us at any one time, it's breathtaking, it's unreal how many planes are in the air at any given time.

Very good app, let's you see planes and their routs for free unlike other apps such as flightradar that you need to pay to see those things. With flightaware you get almost every features for free and the ads are there but not bad, no popup ads just inbeded ads same as that other apps like Flightradar, but that app only gives you access to very basic info {Arrival, Departure Times, Type of Aircraft, Destination, Departed From, Google's Satellite Map } only basic info.

This app is my go-to for all travel plans, whether it's for myself or visitors. Not only can you see the status for your flight, you can see the status of the inbound flight that will become your plane. A lifesaver when the airline says your flight is delayed and you can see the real-time truth. For my visitors, I can see if their plane really took off, since the airline sometimes says departed even if the plane is sitting on the tarmac. The app also has all sorts of fun stats for flights.

This is a terrific app for monitoring airports, aircraft, routes and the like. It's extremely useful and, I must say, fun! I had to contact Support to find out how to delete a search and they responded within hours with how to do it (long press on the search criteria.) Great app and great support!

Must have for any frequent flyer I check the weather before flying. I check where the plane is if it is late getting to the gate. I check the conditions at the airport where I will be landing. Yesterday, case in point, my flight was delayed. My plane was flying in from Midway and hadn't arrived. Flights out of Denver were being delayed 1 hour and 8 minutes due to weather. If I had needed to adjust my travel plans, I would have had all the info I needed.

very helpful. The Denver Airport kidnapped me for a day. My son used this app to keep up with my second flight from Denver to Greensboro. BTW- if you are looking for a flight and a cheap one comes up? If you have a layover in Denver, resist the urge to book it. They couldn't pay me to go through Denver. I've had 3 separate flights through that airport and 3 nightmare connections . Pay more and skip Denver.

Awesome addition to my phone :-) nothing cooler than seeing whats in the sky above me when Im curious! Thank u so much 4 this great app! It barely has any ads at all and if there is an ad it doesn't interfere with what you are doing. I first downloaded this app in the middle of the night when I was curious about a small engine plane that just kept flying in circles. Its easy to work and tells a lot of info about the plane . Click on the plane you want and it shows utype,altitude&speed

The best flight tracker. I mean, all i want is to see *ACCURATE* information, and this is the only one that hasn't let me down. Note that im leaving review after using a competitors product that cost me a lot of money by displaying outdated information. So... well, now i only trust flightaware.

About a year ago was acclaimed by other pilots I met with as the best flight following app, and I'd agree. I did not rate it less than 5 stars because the reason it may be constantly stopping of recent may have to do with my phone. I forced stop, emptied the caches on all the apps and restarted, but still no joy (will not show flight progress map) . Although my SM-J337 (Android V9) is somewhat dated, I'd hate to upgrade only to experience the same problem on a new phone.

Good for alerts, good to know when it's time to head to the airport, good to know if i must hurry up or not. For frequent flyers, it' s way better than other apps. The alert system is way advanced yet very easy to program. I'm also a feeder (piAware, fr24 and adsbExchange) yet, this is the app of choise! Thanks

Thank you. Much better. It does what I need, which is to identify passing planes. Only downside? After looking at a flight's details, it returns to local view rather than the location of the flight being looked at. Example, looking at flights from West Coast to Toronto over Saskatchewan will always return to the Bruce Peninsula.

One of the greatest information travel apps in history. It is so great to be able to see where the plane you will board is actually located. So often gate agents fail to inform passengers of delays. But when you can look and see that the inbound plane still hasn't even taken off, you don't have to believe the agent saying it still shows on time. There is a special place in Heaven for the creators of this app. Simply awesome.

its THE BEST to track a flight in real time. i also love that it gives the height and speed of plane. tells how long the flight is and how much time is left. Its the very best!!!! Will not use anything else!!!
Great app. ans a must for frequent flyers! Easy to use.
I am an a tour guide. thus apps makes my work easier

The absolute BEST, BEST, BEST flight tracking app available. I've been using it for years to track family and friends when traveling. The app is very well organized, extremely easy to use and wonderful.! Thank you folks at FlightAware for this app to allow me to track my loved ones while traveling.
